subject: [PATCH v2 10/13] nfsd: add netlink upcall for the svc_export cache

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a redundant attribute set in the netlink header and agreed to remove it, pushing a fixed patch to their 'exportd-nl' branch.
reviews.raw_body:
No, good catch.

That's a holdover from an earlier version that had a separate attribute
set for the up and downcalls. It's not needed in the latest version.

It should be fine to remove it and regenerate the headers. The result
builds fine without it. I've pushed a fixed patch to the 'exportd-nl'
branch in my tree. You can fix it up or I can resend if you prefer.

Thanks,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever suggested waiting for additional comments before proceeding, indicating a neutral sentiment.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for jumping on that. Let's wait to see if there are other review
comments.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-03-25.patch_summary: This patch adds netlink-based cache upcall support to the NFS daemon (nfsd) for its svc_export cache. It introduces a new netlink event, NFSD_CMD_CACHE_NOTIFY, which is sent when there are pending requests in the cache. The event carries information about the cache type and allows userspace to handle the notifications. The patch also includes changes to the nfsd code to wire up the notification mechanism and update the cache based on user responses.
